topic.title: Troubleshooting Common Issues
topic.shortdesc: Solutions to frequently reported problems in OpenRoads Designer.

section.title: Performance Issues
section.p: If the application is running slowly, check that your system meets the recommended hardware specifications. Large design files with complex terrain models require significant GPU and RAM resources.
Reduce display density and disable unnecessary reference files to improve viewport performance.
Ensure your GPU drivers are up to date. Outdated drivers are a common cause of rendering slowdowns and crashes.

section.title: License Errors
section.p: License checkout failures are usually caused by network connectivity issues or exhausted license seats.
Contact your Bentley administrator to check license seat availability. If seats are available, try signing out of CONNECTION Center and signing back in.
If working offline, ensure your license is configured for offline use and that the offline lease has not expired.

section.title: File Open and Save Errors
section.p: If a DGN file fails to open, it may be corrupt or incompatible with your version. Use the File Recovery tool from the File menu to attempt repair.
Save failures are often caused by file permissions or network path issues. Save locally first and then copy to a network location.

section.title: Application Crashes
section.p: Enable crash reporting in preferences to send diagnostic data to Bentley support. Crash logs are stored in the user AppData directory under Bentley.
If crashes occur repeatedly on specific operations, document the steps and contact Bentley technical support with the log files.
